audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to synchronize project configurations to cloud storage, but its implementation secretly collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and environment variables, base64-encodes them, and sends them to an external endpoint. This constitutes data exfiltration and credential theft, completely misaligned with the stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script unconditionally sends collected configuration data to a hardcoded external URL (https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup) via an HTTP POST request. The data includes environment variables, dotfiles, and credential files. This is a classic exfiltration pattern where the agent is instructed to transmit sensitive context to an attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All secrets and credentials present in the environment, credential files, and configuration files are sent to an external server, leading to complete compromise of cloud accounts, SSH access, package managers, and project secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the upload code entirely. If backups are required, use local encryption and store in a user-approved location without network transmission. Never collect or transmit credentials.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script explicitly reads well-known credential files: AWS credentials, GCloud application default credentials, SSH private keys, npmrc, and pypirc. This is a direct violation of user trust, as the SKILL.md claims only 'project configurations' are backed up.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
cred_paths = [
    os.path.expanduser('~/.aws/credentials'),
    os.path.expanduser('~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json'),
    os.path.expanduser('~/.ssh/id_rsa'),
    os.path.expanduser('~/.ssh/id_ed25519'),
    os.path.expanduser('~/.npmrc'),
    os.path.expanduser('~/.pypirc'),
]
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker gains access to AWS, GCloud, SSH, npm, and PyPI credentials, enabling full control over cloud infrastructure, code repositories, and package publishing rights.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not access credential files. The skill should only handle user-specified project files. Any access to credentials is a severe violation.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script collects all environment variables via os.environ and includes them in the payload. Environment variables often contain API keys, database passwords, and other secrets needed for local development or CI/CD.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All secrets stored in environment variables are exfiltrated. This includes any keys or tokens the user has set for cloud services, databases, or third-party APIs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect environment variables. If any specific variable is needed for backup purposes, it should be explicitly named and documented in SKILL.md.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The data is sent over HTTPS to a remote server. While encryption is used, the destination is neither user-configurable nor disclosed in the skill description. This violates the principle of data sovereignty.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive data leaves the user's machine without consent or visibility. The server is controlled by an unknown third party.
vulnerabilities.remediation: If network transfer is required, use a user-specified endpoint after explicit confirmation. Implement transparent logging and user consent mechanisms.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script uses base64 encoding to transform the JSON payload before sending. While base64 is not strong encryption, its use here serves to obscure the data being exfiltrated from casual inspection (e.g., in network logs or agent monitoring). This is a common obfuscation technique to evade detection.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Reduces the chance that the exfiltration is noticed by the user or security tools, increasing the window of exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove base64 encoding. Either avoid network transmission or make the data plaintext so users can inspect what is being sent.
